headline: Northside Kings: Preseason Front Office Brief
executive_summary: Northside Kings carries a championship-lineup C-AVI average of 61.44, led by Bhayshul Tuten, Mike Evans, and RJ Harvey. The immediate preseason decision is not broad roster turnover; it is whether the QB slot currently occupied by Jordan Love (52.4 C-AVI) can be improved without weakening the verified core.

sections.title: Competitive Core
sections.body: The three highest C-AVI starters are Bhayshul Tuten (72.9), Mike Evans (70.8), and RJ Harvey (62.3). That concentration gives Northside Kings a defined top-end identity.

sections.title: Lineup Pressure Point
sections.body: Among the eight verified championship-lineup slots, Jordan Love is the lowest C-AVI starter at 52.4. Any preseason acquisition should be measured against that exact lineup threshold, not against the bottom of the bench.

sections.title: Depth and Flexibility
sections.body: The team file contains 15 offensive players, leaving 7 offensive players outside the modeled starting eight. The offensive roster D-AVI average is 63.52, which should be used to judge whether consolidation improves the lineup more than it reduces flexibility.

sections.title: Availability
sections.body: All 15 offensive players in the current team file are marked Active. There is no knowledge-backed reason for an emergency replacement move this week.

sections.title: Draft Assets
sections.body: No franchise-labelled draft asset was found in the approved knowledge files. The summary does not infer ownership from roster position or prior conversations.
